text.title: browser-automation Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for browser-automation as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Using page.waitForTimeout(2000) or sleep() as the primary wait strategy.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Fixed timeouts are either too short (flaky on slow CI) or too long (slow overall); condition-based waits are correct and faster.

failure-mode.description: Generating selectors from imagined HTML without feeding the agent actual DOM or accessibility tree.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: LLMs invent plausible-looking selectors that don't match the real page; feeding page.content() prevents this.

failure-mode.description: Spawning N browsers in retry loops without a concurrency cap, causing OOM on the host.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Each headless Chromium instance consumes ~50-100MB RAM; uncapped retries exhaust memory within minutes.

failure-mode.description: Using CSS class selectors (e.g., .bg-blue-600) in automation scripts on Tailwind-styled sites.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Tailwind JIT classes change on refactors; role= and text= selectors survive styling changes.

failure-mode.description: Reusing one browser context across multiple scraping tasks, causing auth state leakage.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Cookies and localStorage persist; auth from task A bleeds into task B, causing wrong-user actions.

failure-mode.description: Automating payment, account creation, or destructive form submission without human-in-the-loop confirmation.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Irreversible actions must be confirmed by a human seeing the rendered form before submission.
